no DB/proxy observed; static JPEG assets under /static. SURFACE: GET / and /index.php render login + read Cookie: creds; POST /index.php accepts username,password and sets creds cookie to base64(serialize(array(...))); GET /static/*.jpg/png serves images; /robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz 404. BUG-HYP: src/index.php lines with $auth = unserialize(base64_decode($_COOKIE['creds'])) and if ($auth['username'] == 'admin' && $auth['password'] == $password) use user-controlled serialized cookie plus PHP loose == comparison. WHY: The cookie is client-controlled and directly unserialized; auth check compares array fields with ==, not ===. In PHP, a boolean true value injected in serialized password should compare equal to any non-empty password string, while username can be exact string admin. BREADTH VECTORS RANKED: (1) PHP type juggling in serialized cookie; (2) cookie tampering to set known password if source static password matches live; (3) PHP object injection via unserialize if classes/autoload existed; (4) source/backup exposure such as index.php~/.bak; (5) static asset stego/metadata; (6) Apache/PHP old-version CVEs/misconfig; (7) path traversal/LFI (no file params seen).
